task_completion.description: Agent imported the CSV reference data file using File > Import and mapped the coordinate columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An import dialog or data wizard is visible showing giza_reference_points.csv selected, or imported placemarks appear in the sidebar under 'Temporary Places' with names like 'Khufu Pyramid Center'.

task_completion.description: Agent created 'Giza Plateau Survey 2025' folder with subfolders 'Pyramid Complex' and 'Supporting Structures'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Places sidebar shows a folder named 'Giza Plateau Survey 2025' with visible child folders 'Pyramid Complex' and 'Supporting Structures'. The Add Folder dialog may be visible with one of these names typed.

task_completion.description: Agent navigated to the Giza Plateau showing the three pyramids in satellite imagery.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Satellite imagery shows three distinct square or diamond-shaped pyramid structures in a desert landscape adjacent to an urban area (Cairo). The pyramids cast clear diagonal shadows.

task_completion.description: Agent created placemarks for the three pyramids with names and descriptions containing dimension data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Placemark pins are visible on or near the three pyramids. The New Placemark dialog or sidebar shows names containing 'Khufu', 'Khafre', or 'Menkaure'. Description text containing measurements like '230m', '215m', or '108m' is visible in the dialog.

task_completion.description: Agent used the Ruler tool to measure distances between pyramids.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ruler dialog is open with a measurement line drawn between two pyramid locations. A distance value in the range of 300-600 meters is displayed in the ruler results.

task_completion.description: Agent created placemarks for the Great Sphinx and Valley Temple of Khafre in the 'Supporting Structures' subfolder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Placemark pins are visible east of the main pyramids, near the Sphinx area. The dialog or sidebar shows names 'Great Sphinx' or 'Valley Temple'. Description text referencing dimensions or the causeway connection is visible.

task_completion.description: Agent created a path named 'Khafre Causeway' with multiple waypoints connecting the Valley Temple area to the Pyramid of Khafre.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line (path) is visible on the map running from the area near the Sphinx/Valley Temple northwest toward the Pyramid of Khafre. The New Path dialog or sidebar shows 'Khafre Causeway'. Multiple vertex points are visible along the line.

task_completion.description: Agent set terrain exaggeration to 2.0x and configured a tilted 3D perspective view showing the pyramids.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Options/Preferences dialog shows elevation exaggeration set to 2 or 2.0, OR the final map view shows a tilted 3D terrain rendering with pyramids and landscape in perspective rather than a flat top-down view.

task_completion.description: Agent exported the survey folder as KML to /home/ga/Documents/giza_plateau_survey.kml.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Place As' or 'Save File' dialog shows 'giza_plateau_survey.kml' being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/', or the agent right-clicks a folder and selects 'Save Place As'.
A 'Save Image' dialog shows 'giza_overview.png' being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/', or the agent uses File > Save > Save Image with the correct filename visible.

integrity.description: Agent used Google Earth Pro GUI, not scripting or a terminal to generate KML code.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Google Earth Pro interface with toolbars, 3D globe, and Places panel. The agent is NOT using a text editor or terminal to write XML/KML code.

integrity.description: Placemarks and paths were created through GUI dialogs by interacting with the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using Add > Placemark and Add > Path dialogs, clicking on the map, typing names and descriptions in dialog fields. Not pasting pre-written KML blocks.

integrity.description: Measurements came from the Ruler tool, not from pre-known values typed directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Ruler dialog is visible with a measurement line drawn on the map between two pyramid locations. The displayed value corresponds to a genuine measurement.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Giza Plateau coordinates: Great Pyramid of Khufu at 29.9792N, 31.1342E; Pyramid of Khafre at 29.9761N, 31.1308E; Pyramid of Menkaure at 29.9725N, 31.1281E; Great Sphinx at 29.9753N, 31.1376E; Valley Temple at 29.9741N, 31.1378E. Distance Khufu to Khafre is approximately 476m. Distance Khafre to Menkaure is approximately 477m. The Ruler tool should show values in the range of 400-500m for these measurements. The Khafre Causeway runs from the Valley Temple (near the Sphinx, east side) northwest uphill to Khafre's pyramid, approximately 495m long (Wikipedia: 494.6m). The three pyramids are arranged in a rough diagonal line from northeast to southwest. In satellite imagery the pyramids appear as square shapes with shadow lines, adjacent to Cairo's urban sprawl. The CSV at /home/ga/Documents/giza_reference_points.csv has 8 reference points with columns: point_id, name, latitude, longitude, type, notes.
